title: Did the Second Impact alter Mankinds sanity
display_title: Did the Second Impact alter Mankinds sanity
wikitext:
[]: Did the Second Impact alter Mankind's sanity?             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sat Apr 14, 2012 11:09 pm I was beginning to think about the end of the Graphic Novel Watchmen, which (without giving too much away to the three of you who haven't read it) involved a number of people around the world having panic attacks and night terrors upon seeing and hearing about the climactic event. I was thinking that the same sort of thing could be applied to the Second Impact. Obviously, it killed a hell of a lot of people and warped the landscape of the Earth. But what if Adam's brief return did more than that? What if it caused mass hysteria and insecurity among those who survived? As it's been shown in the series, many of the characters have mild to severe mental issues (primarily the latter). Shinji has no self-esteem, Rei has no identity, Asuka is unable to trust anyone, Misato is unable to commit to anyone, Ritsuko is a borderline sociopath, and Gendo has a god complex. Furthermore, many of the less-prominent characters in the story seem to have their own issues. Toji, for instance, beats the everloving hell out of Shinji simply because he hurt her sister (when the fact of the matter was that many more people could have been injured/killed had it not been for Shinji's actions). Aoba is shown as being completely nihilistic (to the point that his Instrumentality revealed that he loved nothing). Yui's even implied to be pretty unhinged herself (especially in the movie). The Third Impact would also seem to support this theory. Shinji looking at Giant Naked Rei alone was enough to send him into a shrieking fit of madness. Many people seem yearning to die (with the aforementioned exception of Aoba), and as it's been noted before, many of the screams during the whole "world transforming into a graveyard of souls" sound like the people are experiencing pleasure from their destruction. So, I ask again: Did the Second Impact alter Mankind's sanity?

[]:             Quote Post by Lorkhan » Sun Apr 15, 2012 12:22 am This is a very small sample size in comparison to the rest of the world. The main problem here is if this were true, I doubt Man would be able to hold themselves together post-second impact if this were the case. Also, all these characters have problems because, well, its for the purpose of telling a story. Introducing how many characters with normal and fully functional lives is perhaps refreshing, but it makes for only marginal character development.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Apr 15, 2012 12:47 pm I would think every point Evangelion wanted to make about humanity would be rendered meaningless if the humanity we saw was a fundamentally different psychological species, so definitely not.

[]: Re: Did the Second Impact alter Mankind's sanity?             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sun Apr 15, 2012 2:45 pm Henry Spencer wrote: As it's been shown in the series, many of the characters have mild to severe mental issues (primarily the latter). Shinji has no self-esteem, Rei has no identity, Asuka is unable to trust anyone, Misato is unable to commit to anyone, Ritsuko is a borderline sociopath, and Gendo has a god complex.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:01 pm To clarify, I'm not saying that 2I turned people into raving Paranoid Schizophrenics or anything. I'm just wondering if it has adverse effects against people's minds.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:13 pm Henry Spencer wrote: To clarify, I'm not saying that 2I turned people into raving Paranoid Schizophrenics or anything. I'm just wondering if it has adverse effects against people's minds.

[]: Re: Did the Second Impact alter Mankind's sanity?             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Apr 19, 2012 12:37 pm driftking18594 wrote: Asuka is unable to trust anyone because her mother killed herself and no-one offered her any psychological support.

[]: Re: Did the Second Impact alter Mankind's sanity?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 19, 2012 3:05 pm Henry Spencer wrote: Furthermore, many of the less-prominent characters in the story seem to have their own issues. Toji, for instance, beats the everloving hell out of Shinji simply because he hurt her sister (when the fact of the matter was that many more people could have been injured/killed had it not been for Shinji's actions). Aoba is shown as being completely nihilistic (to the point that his Instrumentality revealed that he loved nothing). Yui's even implied to be pretty unhinged herself (especially in the movie).

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Thu Apr 19, 2012 6:25 pm I think Sharaz was referring to Rei's abandoned apartment complex. That would make sense. Aoba's nihilistic, but you don't really see it anywhere since the NERV workers aren't really fleshed out.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Thu Apr 19, 2012 6:30 pm Henry Spencer wrote: I think Sharaz was referring to Rei's abandoned apartment complex. That would make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 19, 2012 6:44 pm Henry Spencer wrote: Aoba's nihilistic, but you don't really see it anywhere since the NERV workers aren't really fleshed out.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Thu Apr 19, 2012 7:42 pm Na7e wrote: Rei has the soul of an Alien God. Who also wants to die. Has the social skills of an Autistic Kindergartener. And, it's implied she reeks of blood, so her being hygienic is rather laughable, it has nothing to do with it making it a masoleum when she just doesn't give a fuck how it looks.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu Apr 19, 2012 10:41 pm mankinds always been crazy both in and out of eva. I don't think second impact changed anything in regards to that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Fri Apr 20, 2012 10:49 am Henry Spencer wrote: I think Sharaz was referring to Rei's abandoned apartment complex. That would make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Fri Apr 20, 2012 11:24 am Sharaz Destler wrote: No, I was referring to Misato's apartment complex. Hers seems to be the only one with the lights on throughout the series. Of course, it also holds for Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 20, 2012 12:16 pm Na7e wrote: Wow, your basing off a ridiculously small sample size out of two billion people.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Apr 20, 2012 2:00 pm Infact, Yui did what she did in an attempt to basically save all mankind, and Kyoko had her soul violated and ripped in half. None of the damage done to their kids is really their fault whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 20, 2012 2:44 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Infact, Yui did what she did in an attempt to basically save all mankind, and Kyoko had her soul violated and ripped in half. None of the damage done to their kids is really their fault whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by valshon » Sun Apr 22, 2012 3:00 am look at middle America after the cold war came about, everyone was all about duck and cover, we all might die in a firey nuclear hell at any moment. Look at the aftermath of 9/11, everyone was in constant fear of a terrorist attack. Im positive that something even more massive than what I described would completely change ones outlook on life as they had known it before.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Apr 22, 2012 6:10 am I have two things to say in this discussion. Sharaz Destler wrote: I think I can say that Second Impact and everything that came after it made everyone go mad in that it made everyone stop caring about their children .
